text:
Accurate probabilistic prediction is often as important as accurate classification in many AI applications. While many models are very discriminative they often produce outputs that are poorly suited for a probabilistic interpretation. Bayesian Binning into Quantiles (BBQ) is a novel non-parametric calibration approach that extends the benefits of binning and scaling methods while avoiding their main shortcomings.

BBQ is a highly flexible post-processing procedure that can be used in conjunction with any binary classification algorithm. Unlike standard histogram binning which divides the range of values into fixed-sized bins, the Bayesian BBQ method uses Bayesian model averaging to combine the predictions from multiple binning regimes, resulting in a more robust procedure that is less sensitive to the choice of bin width and that handles more unusual distributions in the data. Experiments are reported on a variety of real-world and simulated test sets for several different classification algorithms and show that BBQ yields accurate and reliable posterior probabilities very efficiently, significantly out-performing standard competitors such as Platt scaling and isotonic regression.
